name: hcportal_getKennel
description: Retrieves comprehensive details for a single kennel (hash running club) by its public ID. Returns kennel profile, location info, website settings, payment configuration, default run settings, and related geographic data. Used by both admin portal and public HashRuns.org sites.

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: The user's public hasher ID. Two service account GUIDs are special: 11111111-... bypasses admin check (@isAdmin = 0), 22222222-... allows admin access. All others are validated against HC.Hasher.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN. The @publicKennelId parameter is passed as the kennelId argument to the token check function.

parameters.name: publicKennelId
parameters.description: The public kennel ID to retrieve. Required. Validated by DATALENGTH check (must be 16 bytes).

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Used in LOG.GeneralLog message field. Falls back to '<no IP address>' if NULL.

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Stored in LOG.GeneralLog data field. Falls back to placeholder JSON with '??' country code if NULL.

rowsets.description: Uses errorType codes 2 (validation: null/invalid publicHasherId, hasher not found, null/invalid publicKennelId, kennel not found) and 3 (auth: invalid access token). Multiple error rowsets possible if validation doesn't short-circuit. Each validation block has '@isError = 0' guard but doesn't RETURN, so multiple errors may be logged.

rowsets.name: KennelDetailResult
rowsets.description: Returned on success after validation and logging. Contains comprehensive kennel profile with 70+ columns including geography (city/region/country/continent), website customization, payment schemes (x3), bank details, dissemination settings, default run configuration, and feature flags.

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation/auth failures (lines 37, 59, 77, 98, 117)
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call after initial validation (lines 136-147), includes IP address and geo details, with different LogSource prefix based on @isAdmin flag

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present (line 19) but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback.
The entire SP has no error handling. If the final SELECT fails (e.g. bad JOIN), the error propagates unhandled to the caller.

All validation blocks run regardless of prior failures. Each failed check SELECTs an error rowset and has an '@isError = 0' guard for subsequent checks, but does not RETURN until line 151. If multiple validations fail, the caller receives multiple error rowsets before the single RETURN. The final SELECT (line 154) never executes when @isError = 1, but all validation errors are already returned.
Lines 31, 72: Uses DATALENGTH instead of checking for NULL alone. For UNIQUEIDENTIFIER, DATALENGTH will always return 16 bytes if not NULL, making the check redundant. Should just check 'IS NULL'.

codeSmells.issue: Hardcoded magic GUIDs for service accounts
codeSmells.detail: Lines 49, 130-133: Two special GUIDs (11111111-... and 22222222-...) bypass normal hasher validation and control @isAdmin flag. The 11111111-... GUID sets @isAdmin = 0 for HashRuns.org public access. No documentation of what each represents.

codeSmells.issue: @isAdmin set but never used for access control
codeSmells.detail: Lines 129-133: @isAdmin flag is set based on publicHasherId value and used ONLY in the LOG.GeneralLog LogSource prefix (line 143). It does not control what data is returned or enforce any access restrictions. Dead logic for access control.
Lines 13-14, 144-145: These parameters are accepted and used in LOG.GeneralLog but have no impact on SP logic or output. Consider if they should be optional or document their logging-only purpose.
Lines 21, 23, 28: Multiple commented-out debug/test EXEC statements with real GUIDs and JSON payloads. Leftover from development/debugging.
Line 166: 'GoogleCalendarId' column is commented out in the final SELECT but the column exists in the table. Unclear if this is intentional deprecation or temporary removal.
Line 203: Output column is 'canEditRunAttendence' but should be 'canEditRunAttendance'. Typo in the SP; fixing in HC6 would be a breaking change unless clients tolerate both spellings.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
Never fix the 'canEditRunAttendence' typo without coordinating with all clients
